item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from Wikimedia Commons under CC BY-SA 4.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd work with an army of immortal, battle-hardened Vikings too. I mean, what are you going to do? They're gonna get back up, take a draft of mead and praise the Valfodr for giving them such tenacious and awe-inspiring opponents. Then there's the chance you might insult said Father of the Slain. Which would probably end badly, what with the fact we've found the World-Serpent. He might ask Thor to stay his hand at Ragnarok long enough for it to give the Foundation a good thrashing. You know. Out of spite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the thing I like most about this is that the Foundation is actually working with an SCPs governing body to contain it rather than just taking over and locking it in a cage. I know it's really nothing new, but I like the flavor none-the-less.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loving the concept of post-life bureaucracy. Just because you die doesn't mean you can't still make bad choices and ruin your life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love Old Norse culture and know a fair bit about it, and that's possibly why I find this one entirely too straightforward. Realistically speaking, I suppose the Foundation would have to contain some straight-up haunted places, but that doesn't mean it makes for interesting reading. It's stylistically competent, which is the minmum I'd expect from your stuff— but the idea just doesn't grab me. I dunno. Maybe on a reread I'll neutralize my vote, but for now it just seems frustratingly toneless. If it had a bit more atmosphere to it maybe the simplicity of the idea wouldn't stand out as a bad thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice detail in the containment procedures, and overall , neat article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Smapti, you just know more things than I do.

There is no SCP-1987-1 in this article.

My original point being that I don't see why there shouldn't be. "Specimens" is just… odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way too long, especially the containment procedures contain history about the SCP that should be moved elsewhere. Maybe take out a lot of the side notes and instead put them as addendums. Transcripts of the "Valthings" and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept of an anomaly that is contained through diplomacy, so I upvoted. I do agree there's not enough payoff for the buildup, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why do the Norwegians need their own version of the Foundation?

Because there wasn't a Foundation version of the Foundation in the 19th century.

Why is its name italicized (or for that matter, translated)?

Italicizing and translating foreign terms presumed to be unfamiliar to the speaker is standard practice in written English.

Calling them "specimens" seems odd. I would more expect "instances of SCP-1987-1" or somesuch.

There is no SCP-1987-1 in this article.

What does "medieval football" even mean?

It means the type of football that was played in the medieval era.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why do the Norwegians need their own version of the Foundation? Why is its name italicized (or for that matter, translated)?

Calling them "specimens" seems odd. I would more expect "instances of SCP-1987-1" or somesuch.

What does "medieval football" even mean?

There's a lot of stuff packed into this, but I think I'll agree that the payoff isn't really worth it, though it certainly is by no means a boring read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as the Swede in me loves Norse content, this was such a long read to get to a cross between Brigadoon and Valhalla.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not enough Norse stuff on the site. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but it's a bit too long for a relatively simple concept, and could use some pruning to get it to a better length. The addendum about the individual getting removed from the group is interesting but kind of unnecessary, for example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder if someone's been watching Vikings on History Channel. >.>

I'm going to have to lean on the side of this being too vanilla and straight-up adapted mythology to really hold my interest. To be fair, though, I'm also somewhat biased.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article drags on and on and I had to fight to get my eyes to focus on the words by the end. I quite like the idea, to be honest (the afterlife being boring is quite a thought-provoking theme) but the way this is written is too drawn-out to keep my attention without a proper stinger at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's so much buiidup in the containment procedure, then so much buildup in the description, that you expect it to lead to something. It's just a really, really dull version of Valhalla, and it goes on forever just to make that relatively simple point. Briefness isn't always a virtue, but I feel this article could use it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New article vaguely inspired by the draugr, a term you might best associate with those annoying zombies from a certain game.
